# Implementation Plan: Contract-based CI via local test attestation

**Branch**: `019-contract-ci-json-gate` | **Date**: 2026-05-04 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)  
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/019-contract-ci-json-gate/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was produced by `/speckit.plan`. Phase 2 (`tasks.md`) is generated by `/speckit.tasks`, not this command.

## Summary

Replace merge-blocking reliance on multi-job hosted test workflows with a **manifest + local attestation + single validator job** model: contributors run a documented local command (ultimately exercising the same required bar, including `make ci` per constitution), commit a **JSON manifest** (required checks) and **versioned JSON attestation** (unique run id + generation timestamp + per-check outcomes), and GitHub Actions runs **one** job that **only validates those two committed files** (manifest shape, attestation schema, freshness, FR-007 completeness—no hosted re-run of manifest commands). Clarified scope (**Option A**): hosted CI **does not** re-execute manifest checks for merge; trust boundary and risks are documented (see spec Edge Cases and SC-005).

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python 3.11+ for validator/generator CLIs (align with repo `scripts/ci` and `uv` usage); POSIX shell for thin `make` targets; GitHub Actions YAML for the single gate job.  
**Primary Dependencies**: stdlib `json`, `datetime`, `uuid`; optional `jsonschema` if the repo prefers schema validation via PyPI pin—otherwise minimal hand-validation for v1 to avoid new deps (decision in [research.md](./research.md)).  
**Storage**: Committed **JSON** manifest at `.ci/required-checks.json` and **JSON** attestation at `.ci/ci-attestation.json` (paths normative in spec / quickstart; v1 does not use YAML for either file).  
**Testing**: Contract tests or golden-file tests for validator against fixtures under `specs/019-contract-ci-json-gate/fixtures/` (see **T002**); subprocess contract tests land at **`apis/gateway/tests/contracts/test_ci_attestation_validate_contract.py`** (**T013**) following the monorepo’s existing gateway contract-test layout. Local `make ci` remains the substantive quality bar and must appear as a manifest check.  
**Target Platform**: Linux (GitHub `ubuntu-22.04` runners for validator); developer macOS/Linux for local generation.  
**Project Type**: Monorepo developer workflow + CI gate (Vecinita root `Makefile`, `.github/workflows/*`).  
**Performance Goals**: Validator cold run completes in under 30 seconds excluding checkout (file-only I/O).  
**Constraints**: Exactly **one** workflow job responsible for **manifest + attestation** validation (FR-004); freshness window configurable (default proposed in research); no secrets required for fork PR validation of committed JSON files.  
**Scale/Scope**: One attestation file per PR revision; manifest sized to O(10–10²) check ids.

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-checked after Phase 1 design. Source: `.specify/memory/constitution.md`.*

| Principle | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|--------|
| **Community benefit** | N/A (supporting) | Infra-only; does not change RAG product behavior. Improves explicit merge contract for contributors. |
| **Trustworthy retrieval** | N/A | No corpus or retrieval surface change. |
| **Data stewardship** | N/A | No ingestion/storage change. |
| **Safety & quality** | **PASS** | Manifest **MUST** include `make ci` (or a documented equivalent single target) so constitution “Local CI must pass” remains the substantive bar; attestation proves local execution, with trust boundary documented per spec. |
| **Service boundaries** | **PASS** | Validator reads committed files and Makefile/manifest references only; no new HTTP APIs or cross-service runtime coupling. |

**Post–Phase 1 re-check**: Contracts under `specs/019-contract-ci-json-gate/contracts/` lock attestation and manifest shapes; constitution table unchanged.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/019-contract-ci-json-gate/
├── plan.md              # This file
├── research.md          # Phase 0
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 — JSON Schema + manifest contract
├── tasks.md             # Phase 2 (/speckit.tasks)
├── fixtures/            # Validator golden files: manifest-*.json + attestation-*.json (tasks T002)
└── artifacts/           # Runbooks & evidence (tasks T015–T018): branch-protection checklist, SC trial runbook, SC evidence log
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
.github/workflows/
└── ci-attestation-gate.yml    # NEW: single job — validate committed manifest + attestation (see tasks T009)

scripts/ci/
├── impacted_corpus_test_suites.py   # existing
├── ci_attestation_validate.py       # NEW: schema + freshness + manifest coverage
└── ci_attestation_generate.py       # NEW (optional split): invoke manifest checks, write JSON

.ci/                               # NEW directory (or agreed path)
├── required-checks.json           # NEW: manifest (ids + display names + command mapping index)
└── ci-attestation.json            # NEW: generated; committed per PR update

Makefile                           # NEW targets: e.g. ci-attestation, ci-attestation-validate (local parity)

docs/ (optional)                   # Link from CONTRIBUTING / TESTING_DOCUMENTATION — see quickstart
```

**Structure Decision**: All new automation lives under **`.github/workflows/`** (single gate), **`scripts/ci/`** (Python validator + optional generator), **`.ci/`** (committed manifest + attestation data). **Makefile** exposes contributor-facing commands so `make ci` and attestation refresh stay discoverable.
